title: Phaseoloides brandisianum
taxonomy: Phaseoloides brandisianum is classified within the family of flowering plants known as Fabaceae, commonly referred to as legumes. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics, such as its bean-like pods and compound leaves. The genus Phaseoloides is part of the subfamily Faboideae, which consists of various species of peas and beans. Within the family Fabaceae, Phaseoloides brandisianum is further classified as a member of the tribe Fabeae. This classification is used to group plants with similar characteristics and evolutionary relationships.

intro: Phaseoloides brandisianum, also known as Brandis' climbing bean or climbing sensitive plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of conditions such as fever and arthritis. The bioactive constituents of Phaseoloides brandisianum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties.
